app.title: Requiem of Everything
app.subtitle: An interactive timeline of classical music

nav.item1: Scroll to zoom in/out, centred on your cursor position.
nav.item2: Click and drag to pan across time periods.
nav.item3: On touch screens, drag to pan and pinch to zoom.
nav.item4: Click a composer bar to open their detail panel.
nav.item5: Click a composition dot on the timeline to open its detail card.
nav.item6: Use the + / − / ↺ controls in the top-right corner.
nav.item7: The year range indicator at the top shows your current view.

filter.item1: Open with F or the ⚙ Filters button.
filter.item2: Filter by musical era — Renaissance, Baroque, Classical, Romantic, and Modern.
filter.item3: Filter by nationality — shows only composers from selected countries.
filter.item4: Filter by genre — shows only composers who wrote in at least one selected genre.
filter.item5: Search composers by name or tag.
filter.item6: Toggle historical event markers on or off.
filter.item7: The active filter count badge shows how many filters are on.

comp.item1: Zoom in enough to reveal composition dots on each composer bar, then click any dot to open its detail card.
comp.item2: You can also click a work title in the composer's detail panel to open the same card.
comp.item3: The card shows the title, composer, year, genre, instrumentation, description, and a Wikipedia link if available.
comp.item4: Click the composer's name inside the card to jump to their profile.

compare.item1: Ctrl+click (Cmd+click on Mac) composer bars to add them to comparison.
compare.item2: If a composer detail panel is open, Ctrl+clicking another composer adds both to comparison automatically.
compare.item3: Ctrl+click a compared composer again to remove it.
compare.item4: When 2 or more are selected, non-compared composers dim immediately, then collapse after a few seconds.
compare.item5: On collapse, the timeline auto-zooms to frame the compared composers' combined lifespans.
compare.item6: Hover collapsed bars to expand and identify them.
compare.item7: Clearing comparison restores the original timeline view.
compare.item8: Up to 5 composers can be compared simultaneously.
compare.item9: You can also use the + Compare button in the composer detail panel.
compare.item10: Manage or clear comparisons using the comparison bar in the header.

orchestraExplorer.title: Orchestra Explorer
orchestraExplorer.subtitle: Interactive Orchestral Topography
orchestraExplorer.clickPrompt: Click a section to explore its instruments
orchestraExplorer.stringsDesc: The string family forms the foundation of the orchestra, producing sound through vibrating strings. Seated closest to the conductor, they provide the warm, rich core of the orchestral sound.
orchestraExplorer.woodwindsDesc: Woodwind instruments produce sound by splitting air across an edge or through vibrating reeds. They add colour, agility, and character to the orchestral palette.
orchestraExplorer.brassDesc: The brass family creates sound through buzzing lips into a metal mouthpiece. Capable of tremendous power and brilliant tone, they provide the orchestra's most heroic and triumphant moments.
orchestraExplorer.percussionDesc: Percussion instruments produce sound when struck, shaken, or scraped. Positioned at the back of the orchestra, they provide rhythmic drive, dramatic accents, and colourful effects.
orchestraExplorer.keyboardsDesc: Keyboard instruments add unique textures — from the piano's percussive brilliance to the organ's sustained grandeur and the celesta's bell-like shimmer.
orchestraExplorer.voiceDesc: The human voice is the oldest and most expressive instrument. Vocal soloists and choirs join the ensemble for operas, oratorios, masses, and symphonic works.

welcome.title: Welcome to Requiem of Everything
welcome.description: Discover 500 years of classical music through an interactive timeline. Would you like a quick guided tour?

whatsNew.title: What's New
whatsNew.description: New features have been added since your last visit. Would you like to see what's changed?

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.description: Requiem of Everything is an interactive timeline spanning 1400–1970, covering 51 composers, 196 compositions, and 47 historical events across six musical eras.

timeline.title: The Timeline
timeline.description: This is the main timeline. Scroll to zoom in and out, and drag to pan across centuries. Composer lifespans appear as coloured bars.

timeRuler.title: Time Ruler
timeRuler.description: Year markings and era labels run along the top. The current viewport range is always visible.

eras.title: Musical Eras
eras.description: Colour bands represent the six musical eras — from Renaissance gold to Modern green. Each era has its own palette.

composerBar.title: Composer Bars
composerBar.description: Each bar is a composer's lifespan. Click to see their biography and works. Ctrl+click to compare multiple composers.

composerPanel.title: Composer Profile Panel
composerPanel.description: Click any composer bar to open their profile panel. Here you'll find their biography, notable works listed by year, contemporaries you can jump to, and actions to focus the timeline on their lifespan or add them to a comparison.

composerLinks.title: Wikipedia & External Links
composerLinks.description: Each composer profile includes a link to their Wikipedia article. Compositions — visible when you zoom in enough to see the dots on composer bars — each have their own Wikipedia links and, where available, a Spotify button to listen directly.

compositionDetail.title: Composition Detail Card
compositionDetail.description: Click a composition dot to open its detail card. It shows the year, genre, instrumentation, key, catalogue number, and a description. If available, there's a Wikipedia link and a Spotify button to listen to a recording.

termsView.title: Musical Terms Glossary
termsView.description: The Terms view is a searchable glossary of musical forms, genres, and techniques. Filter by category, search by name, and click any term to see its full definition, era of origin, and example works with Spotify links.

orchestraView.title: Orchestra Explorer
orchestraView.description: The Orchestra Explorer shows the orchestra from above as an interactive map. Click a section — Strings, Woodwinds, Brass, Percussion, Keyboards, or Voice — to explore individual instruments, their role, historical era, and featured compositions.

search.title: Search & Filters
search.description: Search composers by name, and use filters to narrow by era, nationality, or genre. Press F to toggle the filter panel.

views.title: Multiple Views
views.description: Switch between the Timeline, the Musical Terms glossary, and the interactive Orchestra Explorer.

language.title: Language
language.description: The app is fully available in English, French, and Afrikaans. All content — including biographies and descriptions — is translated.

help.title: Help & Shortcuts
help.description: Press ? at any time to open the help panel with keyboard shortcuts, navigation tips, and feature guides.

done.title: You're All Set!
done.description: Start exploring! Click any composer bar to begin, or switch to the Terms or Orchestra views to explore musical vocabulary and instruments. Press ? at any time to open the help panel.

credits.title: Credits
credits.subtitle: The people who built Requiem of Everything
credits.footer: Built with open-source tools including React, D3, Vite, and i18next.
